scheduler/executor.py.summary: Task execution router with dynamic content resolution. Supports {{variable}} templates in payloads that are resolved to live data before sending. Routes to task-specific handlers (telegram, discord, email, api, trade). AI agents add entries to FETCH_DATA_REGISTRY and new task_type handlers with elif routes in execute_task().
key_components: FETCH_DATA_REGISTRY - maps variable names to data fetch functions
execute_task(job) - main entry point called by central scheduler

scheduler/job_manager.py.summary: HTTP client that wraps the backend REST API at /api/scheduler/. This is the LLM's primary tool for creating and managing jobs. All functions call BACKEND_URL/api/scheduler/ endpoints. No direct database access.

services/api_client.py.summary: External API client module. Provides helper functions for public APIs (CoinGecko for crypto, Open-Meteo for weather, Hacker News for news). AI agents add new API functions here. All HTTP requests centralized in this module.

services/web_scraper.py.summary: CDP-based web scraping module using chrome-devtools-mcp. Enables extraction from JS-heavy websites with config-driven selectors, pagination, auth support, and extensible scraper registry.
key_components: WebScraper base class with extraction helpers

config.py.summary: Environment configuration loaded via python-dotenv. Contains PROJECT_ID, PROJECT_PATH, BACKEND_URL (for job_manager), and optional task credentials (TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN, DISCORD_BOT_TOKEN, SMTP_*). No DATABASE_URL — jobs are in main DB.

main.py.summary: Thin entry point. No FastAPI, no database, no scheduler loop. The centralized backend handles all scheduling, job storage, and execution. This file only sets up logging.
